rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 22, Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Memorandum Of Costs. Denied. Notice of Entry of Judgment was served on Plaintiff on August 27. The Memorandum of Costs was served on Plaintiff on August 30 and was therefore timely under Cal. Rule of Court § 3.1700. By filing a proof of service, Defendants met their burden of showing that service was completed. (CCP § 1013(a), "service is complete at the time of deposit in the mail.") The addressee incurs "the risk of the failure of the mail." (Silver v. McNamee (1966) 69 Cal.App.4th 269, 280.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Response To Req. For Production Of Documents, Set No. 2 And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions; Req. To Bypass Hearing By Judge Pro Tem
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 1 - DEFENDANT G. MAZZERA COMPANy's Motion To Compel Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. 2 And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions; Request To Bypass Hearing By Judge Pro Tem. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition to the motion having been filed, the motion is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff shall serve verified code-compliant responses, without objection, to Defendant' Request for Productions of Documents - Set No. 2 and shall also produce all responsive documents in Plaintiff's care, custody, or control no later than December 17, 2013. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant's counsel sanctions in the amount of $800.00 no later than December 17, 2013. Defendant's request to bypass the procedure set forth in Local Rule 10.0(B) for this and future hearings is denied. Defendant has submitted to the court and served on Plaintiff a form of proposed order with Defendant's moving papers, and need not submit a new proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Discovery Orders And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 2 - CROSS COMPLAINANT TOURAJ BOUSSINA, PERRY BOUSSINA's Motion To Vacate Discovery Orders And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. The discovery orders entered on July 9, 2013 and on September 5, 2013 from which Defendants and Cross-Complainants Touraj and Perry Boussina seek relief were validly entered. No appearance were made on the Boussinas' behalf, and pursuant to Local Rule 10.0(B), they are deemed to have stipulated to the motions being decided by a temporary judge. The Boussinas have not demonstrated good cause under Code of Civil Procedure 473(b) for relief from the orders. Mandatory relief under Section 473(b) is not available because the affidavit accompanying Attorney Fried's August 30, 2013 Notice of Unavailability upon which Plaintiff relies does not attest to any mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect, on Mr. Fried's part in failing to respond to the discovery that was the subject of the July 9, 2013 and September 5, 2013 orders. The declarations of the Boussinas themselves also do not fulfill the requirements of Section 473(b) for mandatory relief from the orders since they are not a declaration by their attorney "attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." The August 30, 2013 Fried Declaration also does not demonstrate good cause for discretionary relief from the discovery orders. Mr. Fried avers in the declaration that his significant other suffered a head injury on Friday August 30, 2013 and that he will have to assist his significant other and take over primary care for their baby. All of the discovery responses at issue in the prior orders were due before the date of the accident. Therefore Mr. Fried's declaration does not establish mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or inadvertent neglect in failing to respond to the discovery at issue. The Boussinas' counter-request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY VU CONN VS. SHERRI KING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Sanction For Destruction Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 3 - DEFENDANT SHERRI KING's Motion For A Sanction For Destruction Of Evidence. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO REQUEST AN ADVERSE-INFERENCE JURY INSTRUCTION FROM THE TRIAL DEPARTMENT. A terminating sanction is generally only appropriate in the most egregious cases of repeated efforts of a party to thwart discovery, and often requires violation of a court order. See R.S. Creative v. Creative Cotton (1999) 75 Cal. App. 4th 486, 496. Mr. Vu did not disobey any court orders. Also, while the evidence from Mr. Vu's deposition transcript could support an inference of a desire to suppress evidence, it could also support an inference that the destruction was not a willful attempt to thwart discovery in a case that he did not file until 19 months after the destruction. Violation of a discovery order is not a prerequisite to issue and evidentiary sanctions in exceptional circumstances when the offending party has engaged in a pattern of willful discovery abuse that causes the unavailability of evidence or perhaps if the proponent can demonstrate that obtaining a prior discovery order would be futile. New Albertsons, Inc. v. Superior Court, 168 Cal. App. 4th at 1426 ("Our conclusion that the sanctions are unauthorized does not preclude an instruction that if the jury finds that Albertsons intentionally destroyed evidence, it may infer that the evidence would have been unfavorable to Albertsons. (See CACI No. 204.) Such an instruction is appropriate if there is evidence of willful suppression, that is, evidence that a party destroyed evidence with the intention of preventing its use in litigation. If so instructed, the jury may consider all relevant and admissible evidence in determining whether evidence was willfully suppressed, including the letter requesting the preservation of evidence.") However, the request for such a remedy for spoliation should be brought before the trial department. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UNIGARD INSURANCE COMPANY VS. HARCO NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT THOMAS I. PENDERGAST INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA COAST TRUCK RENTAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Thomas I. Pendergast, Inc., individually and dba Coast Truck Rental's ("Coast") motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative summary adjudication, is denied. The Court finds, as a matter of law, that Unigard Insurance Company ("Unigard") had no legal duty to defend Coast in the underlying wrongful death suit, (Elaine Ike, et al. v. Ford Motor Company, et al., case No. CGC-10-497104), pursuant to Labor Code §3864. This legal finding operates retroactively. (See Scottsdale Ins. Co. v. MV Transp. 36 Cal.4th 643, 655.) Defendants do not dispute that Unigard reserved its rights to seek reimbursement from Coast, or anyone else, for defense costs incurred. (See Joint Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts, 13-14, Exh. 2.) Consequently, Coast and Harco are responsible for reimbursing Unigard for the $323,661.34 in defense costs incurred under equitable indemnity and restitution principles. (See Scottsdale, supra; Clarendon America Ins. Co. v. Mt. Hawley Ins. Co. 588 F.Supp.2d 1101, 1106 (applying California law.)) Unigard is also entitled to prejudgment interest under Cal. Civ. Code §3827. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition Of Michael Bracco, Senior Executive Vice President And Chief Administrative Officer For Bank Of The West, For Protective Order Re Same, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,500
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 4 - DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST's Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition Of Michael Bracco, Senior Executive Vice President And Chief Administrative Officer For Bank Of The West, For Protective Order Re Same, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,500. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide on sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. Under the holding of Liberty Mutual Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal. App. 4th 1282, 1289, when evaluating a request to quash an apex deposition, "the trial court should first determine whether the plaintiff has shown good cause that the official has unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information. If not, as will presumably often be the case in the instance of a large national or international corporation, the trial court should issue the protective order and first require the plaintiff to obtain the necessary discovery through less intrusive methods." Thus, when the deponent has "unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information" a protective order requiring the plaintiff to obtain the necessary discovery through less intrusive methods is not warranted. The undisputed evidence is that Plaintiff sent an email to Bank of the West's CEO, Michael Shepherd (who she had previously met) complaining that she was resigning from the bank because "[f]or several months I had to endure a barrage of insults and sexual harassment from Nolen Boyer . . . " Mr. Shepherd forwarded her email to Michael Bracco with instructions to "Please look into these comments. . . . If you find they are substantiated, please brief me and make any recommendations you believe appropriate." (Stephenson Decl. in Opposition to Motion to Quash, Exhibit K). Plaintiff is entitled to depose Mr. Bracco to determine what actions he took in response to Mr. Shepherd's directive, and what recommendations he made. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C. VS. MELISSA VINCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling Deft'S Responses Without Objections To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Req.'S For Admissions And For Order Deeming All Req.'S For Admissions Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 5 - PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'s Motion For Order Compelling Defendant'S Responses Without Objections To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Req.'S For Admissions And For Order Deeming All Req.'S For Admissions Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiff's proof of service of the motion papers demonstrate service by mail on Defendant's former attorney only, and not on Defendant's current attorney of record, Gregory Brockbank. The evidence submitted by Plaintiff also demonstrates that, prior to the time that Plaintiff filed its motion to compel, Attorney Brockbank informed Plaintiff's counsel that he would be substituting in as counsel for Defendants. (Valonti Decl. Paragraph 5). Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare the order and provide notice of entry of order to Defendants' current counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A. VS. ISRAEL SANTIAGO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Response To Discovery, Order For The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Asserted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 6 - PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A.'s Motion To Compel Response To Discovery, Order For The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Asserted. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant has waived all objections to the discovery by failing to respond. Within 30 days Defendant shall serve verified, code-compliant responses without objections to Plaintiff's first set of special interrogatories and first set of requests for inspection and production of documents, and shall produce all responsive documents in Defendant's care, custody, or control. Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions - Set One are deemed admitted by Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERTREE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. KANIKA KHURANA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issue Sanctions, Evidentiary Sanctions Or To Compel Further Responses To Set One Of Pltf'S Request For Admissions And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft In The Amount Of $1,022
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 7 - PLAINTIFF TENDERTREE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION's Motion For Issue Sanctions, Evidentiary Sanctions Or To Compel Further Responses To Set One Of Plaintiff'S Request For Admissions And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft In The Amount Of $1,022. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. A judicial arbitrator was appointed in this matter after Plaintiff filed its motion, and notice of the appointment was provided to the parties on november 8, 2013. In light of referral of this matter to judicial arbitration, the request for issue or evidentiary sanctions is more appropriately made to the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. TERRI L KNIGHT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Terri Knight'S Mtn To Dismiss Pursuant To Non-Suit Of Subpoena Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 2, 2013, Line 8 - Defendant Terri Knight'S Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Non-Suit Of Subpoena Duces Tecum. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. Defendant has filed an answer to the complaint. Defendant cites a Harvard Law Review article written in 1897 to support her request for a motion to dismiss, but cites no California statute or binding case precedent that would support her motion to dismiss. The court, having found none, DENIES Defendant's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
